_comment: Per-asset size budgets for production dist/. Bytes uncompressed. Matched against dist/assets/ files by stem prefix (vite hashes filenames). A budget can list multiple acceptable stems if vite splits chunks; the script asserts each stem's total bytes <= max_bytes. Total budget asserts the sum across all dist/assets/ files. Update consciously when a planned feature genuinely needs more.

per_stem.note: TypeScript host bundle + style.css. Procedural Canvas2D renderer + WHS croft ports + door-launch + pointer-drive + opt-in music controller + self-hosted font-face CSS. Raised 64→72 KB on 2026-05-27 to reflect music and font features (measured: ~64.2 KB).
WASM kernel from crates/hub-wasm. Houses hub-core sim + boundary.

paint._comment: Paint-timing budgets in milliseconds, asserted against the MEDIAN of N=3 samples per run to absorb single-sample jitter. Measured by scripts/smoke-paint-timing.mjs against `vite preview` running on localhost (chromium-headless via Playwright). These are LAB numbers, not field numbers. `hubFirstFrame` is a User Timing mark fired from src/main.ts inside the first rAF after room.render() — the canvas-aware paint metric the bothy needs because chrome's built-in LCP heuristic collapses to FCP on this canvas-first app (LCP doesn't score the canvas as a contentful element until something paints into it, and the canvas is blank until WASM boots). Calibration: 2026-05-24 tightening sourced budgets from two Linux GitHub-hosted runner samples (CI runs 26350318616 and 26350764592 — 6 sample points per metric). Linux medians observed: fcp=44ms, lcp=44ms, hubFirstFrame=53ms, dcl=27ms, load=28ms. Budgets sit at ~3-5x observed median which absorbs the ~30% sample-to-sample variance seen on shared CI runners while leaving any 2x+ regression to fail loud. Re-tighten when a real CI dataset >= 10 runs is available; loosen ONLY with evidence of a legitimate true-positive that exceeded the budget.
